items.body:
Congrats on taking the first step to contributing to the Sample Programs repository maintained by [The Renegade Coder][renegade-coder]! 
For simplicity, please make sure that your pull request includes one and only one contribution.

Please fill _one_ of the sections below as applicable.
Please also add any other relevant information to the Notes section at the bottom.
You may delete or just ignore any other sections.
For more information please refer to our [contributing documentation][contributing]


## I Am Modifying an Existing Code Snippet or Existing Tests

- [x] I fixed #5497
- [x] I did not include any extra folders/libraries
- [x] I have read about modifying existing code snippets or tests in the [contributing documentation][contributing-modifications]
- [x] I have explained why this modification is _necessary_ either in the issue linked above or in the notes below
- [x] I named the pull request using `Modify {PROJECT} {tests} in {LANGUAGE}` format


## Other Notes

It is wiser to pin the container on the latest LTS, which is JDK 25 right now. I would've switched to JDK 26 as it released a month ago, but I don't want to change the image every 6 months.

[renegade-coder]: https://therenegadecoder.com/
[contributing-plagiarism]: https://github.com/TheRenegadeCoder/sample-programs/blob/master/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md#plagiarism

items.title: feat(java-core): add java-clean-arch skill
items.body:
Covers Clean Architecture / Hexagonal (Ports & Adapters) and DDD:
- Review mode: detects dependency rule violations (domain importing Spring/JPA, business logic in controllers, anemic domain model) and DDD tactical pattern opportunities (value objects, rich entities)
- Implement mode: scaffolds full hexagonal package layout with domain/application/infrastructure separation
- DDD mode: value objects (records for Java 16+), rich domain entities with domain events, port interfaces, persistence adapters

Phased migration path from layered to hexagonal architecture (5 phases, each independently testable).

references/patterns.md contains complete templates for use cases, application services, REST adapters, JPA entities, domain events, and ArchUnit tests to enforce dependency rules.

items.title: feat: add Java-friendly API module (edomata-java-api)
items.body:
## Summary

- Adds `edomata-java-api`, a JVM-only module wrapping Edomata's Scala 3 types into Java-idiomatic classes
- No Scala knowledge required — pure Java usage with `CompletableFuture`, Java collections, and builder patterns
- 15 source files, 56 tests (49 Scala unit + 17-assertion Java integration), comprehensive docs
- Public read-only token for GitHub Packages (`read:packages` only)
- `useReadableConsoleGit` fix for git worktree compatibility

### What's included

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `JDecision` | Accept/Reject/Indecisive with `map`, `flatMap`, varargs factories |
| `JEither` | Simple Left/Right union type |
| `JDomainModel` | Domain model definition (abstract class or factory) |
| `JCommandHandler` | Function-based command handler |
| `JBackendBuilder` / `JBackend` | Fluent builder + facade for Doobie event sourcing |
| `JCodec` | encode/decode interface bridging to BackendCodec.JsonB |
| `JPGSchema` | DDL extraction for Flyway/Liquibase |
| `EdomataRuntime` | AutoCloseable IORuntime lifecycle |
| `JJournalReader` / `JOutboxReader` | FS2 streams → CompletableFuture |

## Test plan

- [x] `sbt javaApi/compile` — 15 source files compile
- [x] `sbt javaApi/test` — 56 tests pass
- [x] Java file (`JavaApiTest.java`) proves API works from pure Java
- [x] Public read token verified: `read:packages` scope only

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
